title: For Immediate Release

raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Disadvantaged Californians would receive a state sales tax break when replacing an older vehicle with an electric or hybrid car.",
    "California aims to have 1.5 million zero-emission vehicles (ZEVs) on the road by 2025.",
    "As of April 30, 2023, only 635,602 ZEVs and hybrid vehicles were registered in California.",
    "Senate Bill 771 would create a new incentive for Californians participating in the Clean Cars 4 All program.",
    "The Clean Cars 4 All program currently offers up to $9,500 in offsets to income-qualified Californians.",
    "SB 771’s incentive would exempt Clean Cars 4 All participants from paying the state’s portion of sales tax when purchasing a new or used ZEV or hybrid car.",
    "Widespread adoption of electric vehicles has been hampered by costs and barriers to entry.",
    "Low-income customers are one of the last groups to adopt the latest technological innovations due to financial constraints.",
    "SB 771 would accelerate California’s environmental efforts by providing tax savings for customers purchasing electric or hybrid vehicles.",
    "The legislation aims to create a more equitable marketplace for EV adoption, especially for low-income customers."
  ]
}
```
